title: sambucus racemosa subsp. manshurica
intro: Sambucus racemosa subsp. manshurica, also known as the Manchurian elder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's berries, flowers, and leaves are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, offering various health benefits such as immune system support, antioxidant effects, and anti-aging properties. The herbal preparations of Sambucus racemosa subsp. manshurica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
